title: A Year of Growth & Privacy: Website Renewal & Switching to Proton
excerpt: Celebrating another year of my personal website and prioritizing privacy with Proton.
content:
It's been an incredible year, and I'm thrilled to announce that I've renewed my website for another year of growth and exploration!

When I first launched spencerallen-dev.github.io, I had big ambitions for what I wanted to share and build. Looking back, I'm proud of the projects I've documented, the posts I've written, and the community I'm building. Whether it's my tinkering with MCP, sharing my Python learning journey, or exploring cyberpunk web design, this site has become a genuine reflection of my tech interests and growth.

With another year ahead, I'm looking forward to:
- Diving deeper into emerging technologies
- Sharing more detailed project breakdowns
- Building increasingly complex tools and experiments
- Connecting with others in the tech community

## Privacy Takes Priority

Along with renewing my site, I've made an important decision to switch to **Proton** for better privacy protection. In today's digital landscape, it's becoming increasingly clear that privacy isn't a luxury—it's a necessity.

I've transitioned to Proton Mail and Proton VPN to ensure that my communications and online activities are encrypted end-to-end. Here's why this matters to me:

**End-to-End Encryption**: Proton Mail encrypts all my emails before they even leave my device. Even Proton themselves can't read my messages. That's the kind of privacy I want.

**No Data Harvesting**: Unlike some mainstream email providers, Proton doesn't scan my emails for advertising purposes or sell my data to third parties.

**VPN Security**: Proton VPN masks my IP address and encrypts my internet traffic, giving me protection on public Wi-Fi and keeping my browsing habits private.

With all the data breaches and privacy concerns we've seen lately, taking control of my digital privacy feels like the right move. It's a small investment in peace of mind.

Here's to another year of building, learning, and staying secure! If you've been thinking about privacy too, I'd definitely recommend checking out what Proton offers.

title: Tinkering with MCP
excerpt: Exploring the Model Context Protocol and building custom tools to extend AI capabilities.
content:
I've been diving into the Model Context Protocol (MCP) lately and it's been a fascinating journey!

MCP allows AI assistants to interact with external tools and data sources in a standardized way. I've been experimenting with creating custom MCP servers that can:
- Access local files and repositories
- Integrate with web APIs
- Provide specialized functionality for specific tasks

The protocol is surprisingly flexible and well-designed. You can build servers in any language that supports JSON-RPC, and the client-server architecture makes it easy to add new capabilities.

I'm working on a few custom tools right now and learning a lot about how AI assistants can be extended beyond their base capabilities. More updates coming soon as I continue tinkering!

title: Welcome to My Blog
excerpt: This is the first post on my new blog. Here I'll share my thoughts on technology, coding, and gaming.
content:
Welcome to my blog! I'm excited to start sharing my journey in tech, coding projects, and gaming adventures. This blog will be a place where I can document what I'm learning, share interesting projects, and connect with others who share similar interests.

Stay tuned for more posts about web development, Python scripting, game modding, and whatever else catches my interest!

title: Building This Website
excerpt: A look at how I built this website using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with a cyberpunk theme.
content:
Creating this website was a fun project. I wanted something that reflected my style - modern, animated, and with a cyberpunk aesthetic.

The key features include:
- Animated particle background using HTML5 Canvas
- Responsive design that works on all devices
- Smooth fade-in animations
- Clean, minimalist layout

I used vanilla JavaScript for the animations to keep things lightweight and fast. The cyan and black color scheme gives it that futuristic feel I was going for.

title: My Python Learning Journey
excerpt: Sharing my experience learning Python and some cool scripts I've created.
content:
Python has become one of my favorite programming languages. Its simplicity and versatility make it perfect for both beginners and advanced projects.

I've been working on various scripts including:
- Automation tools for daily tasks
- Web scraping projects
- Data analysis experiments

The Python community is amazing, and there are so many resources available for learning. If you're just starting out, I highly recommend diving in and building projects that interest you.
